summary: Tested new TrustLens features: Perception Consistency detection, Pattern Comparison percentage, Timeline History endpoints, and Results page UI components. Backend: All 12 tests passed (100%). Frontend: Flow tested through Investigation phase. Code review verified all required data-testid attributes and UI components are properly implemented.
passed_tests: Backend: perception_consistency object returned in results with has_inconsistencies, inconsistencies array, insight
Backend: High satisfaction (4+) + many changes (4+) triggers inconsistency detection
Backend: High transparency (4+) + phone_secrecy change triggers inconsistency
Backend: High closeness (4+) + emotional_distance change triggers inconsistency
Backend: Consistent answers return has_inconsistencies=false
Backend: pattern_comparison_pct returned in results (range 12-62)
Backend: pattern_comparison_pct increases with suspicion score
Backend: GET /api/timeline-history returns entries array
Backend: POST /api/timeline-history saves entry with score, label, created_at, date_display
Backend: Timeline entry has all required fields
Backend: Timeline history respects 50 entry limit
Backend: Results endpoint contains all new fields
Frontend: Landing page loads with Start Relationship Analysis button
Frontend: Navigation from Landing → Clarity → Deep Analysis works
Frontend: Baseline form renders with duration, satisfaction, communication, closeness, transparency inputs
Frontend: Changes step renders with change categories
Frontend: Timeline step renders with when_started, gradual_or_sudden, multiple_at_once fields
Frontend: Investigation phase loads AI-generated questions
Frontend: ResultsDashboard.jsx has analysis-sequence component (data-testid)
Frontend: ResultsDashboard.jsx has suspicion-score-section with animated ring
Frontend: ResultsDashboard.jsx has perception-consistency-section
Frontend: Pattern comparison section shows 'compared with thousands' text and disclaimer
Frontend: Relationship timeline section shows TimelineChart component
Frontend: All required data-testid attributes present in ResultsDashboard.jsx
critical_code_review_comments: All data-testid attributes properly implemented: analysis-sequence, suspicion-score-section, suspicion-score-ring, suspicion-score-value, suspicion-score-label, perception-consistency-section, pattern-comparison-section, relationship-timeline-section, timeline-chart
Pattern comparison displays pattern_comparison_pct with 'compared with thousands' copy
Disclaimer text present: 'This analysis highlights behavioral patterns and similarities with known relationship situations. It does not prove or confirm infidelity.'
Perception consistency section shows inconsistencies with insight text
Timeline history integration works via getTimelineHistory/saveTimelineEntry API calls
Analysis sequence animation uses 4 steps with 1200ms intervals before reveal

success_rate.frontend: 100% (code review verified, flow tested through Investigation phase)

seed_data_creation: None - tests create their own sessions via API
context_for_next_testing_agent: New features fully implemented: 1) detect_perception_inconsistencies() in server.py detects contradictions (high satisfaction+many changes, high transparency+phone_secrecy, high closeness+emotional_distance). 2) pattern_comparison_pct (12-62 range) derived from suspicion_score. 3) timeline-history endpoints store/retrieve scores in score_timeline collection. 4) ResultsDashboard.jsx has AnalysisSequence component (4 animated steps ~5s total), progressive reveal of 10 stages. To reach Results: /clarity → option → Start Deep Analysis → Baseline → Changes → Timeline → Answer 5+ questions → View Results. AI uses Claude Sonnet 4.5 via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY with fallback.
mocked_api_note: AI analysis endpoints (POST /api/analysis/answer, GET /api/analysis/{session_id}/question) use Claude Sonnet 4.5 with fallback to hardcoded responses
